tools.name: minerva_scansort_probe
tools.description: Returns plugin version + a host.echo round-trip result; smoke-test only.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_create_vault
tools.description: Create a new .ssort vault at the given path with the given display name.

path.description: Absolute filesystem path where the new .ssort vault will be created.

name.description: Human-readable display name for the vault.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_set_password
tools.description: Set the encryption password on a vault; writes verifier to project table. Optionally stores a password hint.

path.description: Absolute path to the vault file.

password.description: Password to set on the vault.

hint.description: Optional password hint stored in the vault's project table; surfaced by check_vault_has_password.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_verify_password
tools.description: Verify a password against the stored verifier. Returns {ok: bool}.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_check_vault_has_password
tools.description: Check whether a vault has a password set. Returns {has_password: bool, hint: string}.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_open_vault
tools.description: Open an existing vault and return vault_info.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_update_project_key
tools.description: Update a key/value pair in the project metadata table.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_registry_list
tools.description: List entries in a vault registry.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_registry_add
tools.description: Add a vault to a registry (reads the vault for its name).

tools.name: minerva_scansort_registry_remove
tools.description: Remove a vault from a registry.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_check_sha256
tools.description: Check whether a sha256 exists in a single vault's fingerprints table.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_check_sha256_all_vaults
tools.description: Check whether a sha256 exists in any registered vault.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_insert_document
tools.description: Read a file from disk, compress with zstd, and insert it into the vault's documents table.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_query_documents
tools.description: Query documents with optional filters (category, sender, status, date range, pattern, tag, doc_id).

tools.name: minerva_scansort_get_document
tools.description: Get a single document's metadata by doc_id.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_extract_document
tools.description: Extract a document from the vault to the filesystem, decompressing zstd.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_update_document
tools.description: Update document metadata fields (status, category, display_name, description, tags, issuer, doc_date) by doc_id.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_replace_document_content
tools.description: Replace a document's stored content with a new file, in place. Recompresses, re-encrypts when a password is given, and recomputes sha256/simhash fingerprints. doc_id and all metadata fields are preserved.

vault_path.description: Absolute path to the vault file.

doc_id.description: Document ID whose content is replaced.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the new content file.

password.description: Vault password — required to store the document encrypted at rest. Empty stores plaintext.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_delete_document
tools.description: Hard-delete a document from a vault: removes the documents row and its fingerprints row in one transaction. Irreversible.

vault_path.description: Absolute path to the vault file.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_move_document_to_vault
tools.description: Move a document from one vault to another in memory (no cleartext disk pivot). Decrypts the source with src_password, re-encrypts under dst_password (or stores plaintext if empty), verifies via sha256, then hard-deletes the source. Refuses encrypted→plaintext downgrades unless allow_encryption_downgrade is true.

src_vault.description: Absolute path to the source vault.

dst_vault.description: Absolute path to the destination vault.

src_password.description: Password for the source vault when its document is encrypted.

dst_password.description: Password for the destination vault — non-empty encrypts at rest.

allow_encryption_downgrade.description: Default false. Set true to permit moving an encrypted source into a plaintext destination.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_vault_inventory
tools.description: List all documents in a vault with metadata (no file data blob).

tools.name: minerva_scansort_extract_text
tools.description: Extract text and compute fingerprints from a file (PDF, Excel, Word, PPTX, text, image).

tools.name: minerva_scansort_render_pages
tools.description: Render PDF or image file pages to base64-encoded PNGs for vision classification.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_insert_rule
tools.description: Insert a classification rule into a vault. Returns {ok, rule_id}.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_list_rules
tools.description: List all classification rules in a vault. Returns {ok, rules, count}.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_get_rule
tools.description: Get a single classification rule by label or rule_id. Returns {ok, rule}.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_update_rule
tools.description: Update fields of an existing classification rule by label. Returns {ok}.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_delete_rule
tools.description: Delete a classification rule by label. Returns {ok}.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_import_rules_from_json
tools.description: Bulk-import classification rules from a JSON array string. Returns {ok, count}.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_classify_document
tools.description: Classify a document using LLM via host.providers.chat. Returns {ok, classification}.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_session_open_vault
tools.description: Register an open vault in the plugin's session under a stable label. Panel-side call; LLM agents read state via session_state.

label.description: Stable label to identify the vault in the session.

path.description: Absolute filesystem path to the .ssort vault.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_session_close_vault
tools.description: Remove a vault from the plugin's session by label.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_session_open_directory
tools.description: Register an open directory destination in the plugin's session under a stable label.

label.description: Stable label to identify the directory in the session.

path.description: Absolute filesystem path to the destination directory.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_session_close_directory
tools.description: Remove a directory destination from the plugin's session by label.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_session_open_source
tools.description: Register an incoming source directory in the plugin's session under a stable label.

label.description: Stable label to identify the source in the session.

path.description: Absolute filesystem path to the source directory containing files to classify.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_session_close_source
tools.description: Remove a source directory from the plugin's session by label.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_process_plan
tools.description: C2 (DCR 019e564809a9): Pre-enumerate files that process_run would act on, no LLM/placement work. Installs the result as the CURRENT BATCH; subsequent process_run / process_status / process_cancel reference the batch_id. SCOPE shapes: {kind:'all_sources'} | {kind:'unprocessed_only', vault:'label|path'} | {kind:'explicit_files', files:[...]}. include_paths default false (agent-safe). Returns {ok, batch_id, scope, total, type_breakdown, already_in_vault, eligible, files, created_at}.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_process_status
tools.description: Bug 019e57bbe881 (DCR 019e564809a9): Read-only snapshot of the most recent / current process() run: state, run_id, started_at/finished_at, current_source_label + relpath + index, current_file_total, cancel_requested, last_error, totals {placed, skipped, errored, total}. Stdio caveat: cannot be polled mid-process() on the same connection; useful after process() returns OR when the agent uses limit=1 loop pattern.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_process_cancel
tools.description: G12 (DCR 019e564809a9): Request cancellation of the current process() run via the ProcessController's cancel_requested flag. process() honours it at the inter-file gate. Stdio caveat: cancel only takes effect when the agent runs process() in a polling loop (limit=1). Returns {ok, was_running, last_file_processed}.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_dryrun_session
tools.description: G3 (DCR 019e564809a9): Predict what process() would do across the current session WITHOUT calling the LLM. Per-rule: which copy_to labels resolve to open destinations vs fail. Per-file: filename-driven prediction (LLM-free) — rules whose conditions reference filename/extension/size fire deterministically; rules depending on LLM facts stay silent (predicted_rule_label=null). Catches copy_to label mismatches before paying the LLM cost. Read-only; no state change.

include_paths.description: When true, every file entry carries its absolute path. Default false (agent-safe).

tools.name: minerva_scansort_session_describe
tools.description: G2 + G10 (DCR 019e564809a9): One-stop session shape for agents. Returns labels + per-vault stats + top-level counts. When include_paths=true, also returns destination_registry (full list with is_open_in_session flag) so the agent sees registered-vs-active in one call. Read-only.

include_paths.description: When true, every vault/dir/source entry carries its absolute path. Default false (agent-safe).

tools.name: minerva_scansort_session_state
tools.description: Return the current session — open vaults, directories, and sources. By default returns label-only lists (paths excluded, for LLM-agent safety). Pass include_paths=true (panel-only) to get {label,path} objects. Returns {ok, vaults, dirs, sources}.

include_paths.description: When true, vault/dir/source entries include `path` alongside `label`. Default false (label-only). Panel-side callers set true; LLM agents should not.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_session_reset
tools.description: DCR 019e3d67: drop the entire scansort session back to its initialized state (in-memory only — vault .ssort files, source .scansort-state.json manifests, and the rule library are NOT touched on disk). Clears the labeled vault, directory, and source sets in one shot. Returns {ok, cleared: {vaults: N, dirs: N, sources: N}} with the pre-reset counts.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_clear_source_cache
tools.description: DCR 019e41a5: delete the `.scansort-state.json` source manifest under every currently-open source so the next process() run re-scans those files instead of skipping them. Vault `.ssort` files, vault dedup hash stores, the rule library, and the in-memory session are NOT touched. Returns {ok, attempted, cleared, sources:[{label, manifest_existed, error?}]}.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_library_insert_rule
tools.description: Upsert a rule into the global library (OS app-data path, no vault required). If a rule with the same label exists it is replaced. Returns {ok, rule}.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_library_list_rules
tools.description: List all rules in the global library in stable insertion order. Returns {ok, rules: [FileRule], count}.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_library_get_rule
tools.description: Get a single rule from the global library by label. Returns {ok, rule} if found or {ok:false, error} if not found.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_library_update_rule
tools.description: Apply partial field updates to a rule in the global library by label. Only provided fields are changed. Returns {ok, rule} if found or {ok:false, error} if not found.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_library_delete_rule
tools.description: Remove a rule from the global library by label. Returns {ok, deleted:true} if removed, {ok, deleted:false} if the label was not found.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_library_enable_rule
tools.description: Set enabled=true on a rule in the global library by label. Returns {ok, enabled:true} if found, {ok:false, error} if not found.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_library_disable_rule
tools.description: Set enabled=false on a rule in the global library by label. Returns {ok, enabled:false} if found, {ok:false, error} if not found.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_library_export_to_sidecar
tools.description: Write the current library to the resolved vault's sibling <stem>.rules.json (portable export). Resolves vault_label against the session. Returns {ok, sidecar_path, count}.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_library_import_from_legacy_json
tools.description: G9 (DCR 019e564809a9): One-shot migration from the pre-plugin standalone ScanSort rules file into the global library. Rewrites {sender} → {issuer} placeholders. overwrite_existing controls label-conflict behaviour. Returns {ok, path, imported, skipped, errors}.

path.description: Absolute path to a legacy scansort_rules.json file.

overwrite_existing.description: When true, existing labels are replaced. Default false.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_library_import_from_sidecar
tools.description: Read a vault's sibling <stem>.rules.json and upsert each rule into the library by label (last-write-wins). Resolves vault_label against the session. Returns {ok, sidecar_path, imported, conflicts, total_after}.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_process
tools.description: Run the path-free pipeline against the current session + enabled library rules. Iterates every file in every open source, classifies via host.providers.chat, runs the deterministic rule engine, fans out to destinations whose labels match in session, marks unprocessable files in the source manifest. The flat file list can be windowed with offset/limit for a cancellable per-file loop. Returns {ok, summary, by_rule, by_destination, items, total_files} — total_files is the true pre-window file count.

model.description: Plain model name (e.g. 'gemma4:26b'). Defaults to 'default' which routes to TurnRock/Core. Use model_spec for structured routing.

model_spec.description: Structured provider spec — wins over 'model' when present. Shape: {kind: 'core_action', service_client_id: 'model-chat', action_name: '<model>'} routes via a specific Core service.

doc_type_strategy.description: B8 doc_type normalization strategy. 'enum' = constrain LLM prompt to rule's subtypes. 'canonicalize' = post-LLM alias→canonical mapping. 'both' = enum prompt + canonicalize safety net. Default 'none' (back-compat).

audit_enabled.description: DCR 019e3ce069b6: when true, write one CSV audit-log row per fan-out PlacementResult to `audit_path`. Row shape matches the panel batch pipeline (minerva_scansort_audit_append). Audit failures are non-fatal — the run continues. Default false.

audit_path.description: DCR 019e3ce069b6: absolute path to the CSV audit log file. Required when `audit_enabled` is true; empty path is a silent no-op. Ignored when `audit_enabled` is false.

offset.description: DCR 019e42e4: index of the first source file to process in the flat ordered file list (sources by label, files by relative path). Files before it are skipped cheaply. Default 0.

limit.description: DCR 019e42e4: max files to process starting at `offset`. Omit for the whole batch. The panel's Stop-able loop passes limit=1 per file; total_files in the result stays the true pre-window count.

vault_passwords.description: DCR 019e5068: optional map of destination label → password. When a rule sets encrypt=true, the matching destination's password is used to post-encrypt the just-inserted document via set_document_encrypted. A missing password for an encrypt-flagged destination flips that placement to Error and rolls back the row — process() will NEVER silently store plaintext in an encrypt-flagged destination.

files.description: DCR 019e5068: optional explicit list of absolute file paths to process. When non-empty, the open-source directory walk is bypassed and exactly these files are processed (in supplied order). The .scansort-state.json source manifest is not read or written for this path. Bad/missing paths surface as structured per-file errors. offset/limit still window this list.

tools.name: minerva_scansort_audit_tail
tools.description: G4 (DCR 019e564809a9): Read the last `limit` rows from a CSV audit log written by minerva_scansort_audit_append. Read-only; no state change. Default limit 50, hard cap 1000. Returns {ok, log_path, rows:[{timestamp,event,source_sha256,source_filename,rule_label,destination_id,destination_kind,resolved_path,disposition,detail}], total_rows_returned}. Returns tool_err if the file is missing/unreadable or any row is malformed.

log_path.description: Absolute path to the CSV audit log file. Same path passed to minerva_scansort_audit_append.

limit.description: Max rows to return. Default 50, capped at 1000. Accepts integers or whole-number floats (Minerva's MCP HTTP relay routes ints through Godot, which re-serializes them as floats).

events.name: state_changed
events.description: Emitted after a successful mutating tools/call. payload.kind ∈ {source, destination, session, vault, registry, rule, library_rule, document} tells panels which area changed so they can refresh.
